summary: Decompiled a large portion of src/melee/lb/lbbgflash, replacing many placeholder prototypes and stubs with typed C for background flash rendering, color interpolation, GObj/CObj setup, color animation user data, and several JObj/vector/quaternion helper routines. The PR also tightened lbbgflash.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or loose pointer types to concrete GXColor, HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, Vec3, Mtx, and f32 signatures. The decomp.dev report showed +1448 matched bytes and 5 new full matches, with several larger functions substantially improved but not fully matched.
what_changed: Expanded BgFlashData from loose ints and padding into an offset-annotated struct using BgFlashState, GXColor fields x4/x8/xC, f32 color arrays x10/x20, state/control bytes x30-x33, counters x34/x38/x3C, and HSD_GObj pointers x40/x44.
Changed lbl_804D3840, lbl_804D3844, lbl_804D3848, and lbl_804D384C extern declarations from s32 to GXColor.
Implemented fn_8001FC08 as per-channel RGBA interpolation toward data->x8 using x10 current float color values and x20 deltas.
Implemented fn_8001FEC4 as the GX render callback for background flash quads and strip effects, using GXBegin, GXPosition2f32, GXColor4u8, screen dimensions 640x480, mode checks, and state bytes.
Updated fn_800204C8 to use the typed BgFlashData and GXColor channels rather than the old BgFlashData2 byte overlay.
Implemented lbBgFlash_800206D4 to initialize a color fade between two GXColor values over a clamped frame count.
Changed lbBgFlash_InitState from int* duration-style API to GXColor* and stored the color directly in lbl_80433658.xC.
Implemented fn_800208B0 as a black color/alpha setter for mode 5.

Implemented lbBgFlash_800208EC and completed lbBgFlash_800209F4 GObj/CObj setup paths, including GObj_Create, HSD_CObjLoadDesc, HSD_GObjObject_80390A70, GObj_SetupGXLink(Max), HSD_GObj_SetupProc, gxlink_prios, and state activation.

Implemented matrix/JObj math helpers fn_80020AEC, lbBgFlash_80020E38, fn_8002113C, and lbBgFlash_80021410 with Mtx, Vec3, quaternion, inverse-concat, vector normalization, trig, acos/atan2, and inverse-square-root math.
Added local helper structs IKChainData and BgFlashUserData to describe previously opaque user data layouts.
Implemented lbBgFlash_80021A18 to initialize object allocation/user data, load LbBf.dat symbol lbBgFlashColAnimData, start the flash renderer, and attach fn_80021B04 as a GObj proc.
Implemented fn_80021B04 to update color animation state via fn_80021C80, scale ColorOverlay RGB by lbl_804D63D8, compute alpha from overlay alpha and x0, and pass a GXColor to lbBgFlash_InitState.

Updated lbbgflash.h with concrete prototypes for fn_8001FC08, fn_8001FEC4, lbBgFlash_800206D4, lbBgFlash_InitState, lbBgFlash_800208EC, fn_80020AEC, lbBgFlash_80020E38, fn_8002113C, lbBgFlash_80021410, and fn_80021B04, plus a dolphin/mtx.h include.
smart_moves: Using GXColor for both persistent flash colors and sdata color externs made the RGBA channel accesses explicit and removed fragile byte/int overlays.
Offset comments on BgFlashData and IKChainData preserved layout evidence while still enabling typed field access for matching.
The header cleanup propagated concrete types outward, making later callers less dependent on UNK_RET, UNK_PARAMS, void*, or int* guesses.
The implementation kept unknown field names as x-offset names while improving their types, avoiding unsupported semantic renames.
Matching-sensitive pragmas and padding were applied locally instead of hiding the function logic: PAD_STACK, #pragma dont_inline, and #pragma inline_depth appear around difficult functions.
For quaternion/JObj rotation paths, the PR introduced local fake inline wrappers matching HSD_JObjSetRotation/GetRotation behavior without necessarily changing shared baselib headers.
The code models compiler math patterns explicitly, including __frsqrte plus repeated Newton refinement and volatile temporaries where needed.
For fn_80021B04, byte-level GXColor writes were used where structure-field assignment likely did not match, while comments documented the bytes[1] to bytes[3] intent.
decomp_lessons: For color-heavy rendering code, first identify whether 4-byte words are really GXColor; this often clarifies channel access and improves both C readability and matching.
When a decomp depends on global state, replacing alternate overlay structs with one authoritative offset-annotated struct reduces casts and keeps later functions consistent.
GX immediate-mode drawing often needs exact ordering of GXBegin, position writes, and color writes; even simple full-screen quads can be matching-sensitive.
GObj setup functions are sensitive to temporary variables, slot pointers, and call ordering; preserving intermediate HSD_GObj** slots can matter.
JObj/quaternion routines may need separate code paths for JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION versus non-quaternion rotation storage.
Matrix normalization code may need to mirror the original compiler's inverse-square-root sequence rather than using sqrtf or higher-level vector helpers.
Header prototypes should be upgraded once function signatures are sufficiently proven; this PR shows concrete HSD/JObj/GX types replacing unknown signatures.
Leave evidence comments on awkward matching artifacts, such as unrolled loops, byte assignments, fake assert wrappers, and no-op expressions, so future cleanup does not accidentally break the match.
naming_conventions: Module functions use the lbBgFlash_800xxxxx pattern when exported or module-scoped names are known.
Unidentified local/static-style routines retain address names such as fn_8001FC08, fn_8001FEC4, fn_80020AEC, fn_8002113C, and fn_80021B04.
Struct fields without semantic names use offset-style names such as x4, x8, xC, x10, x20, x30, x34, x40, and x44.
Padding fields are named pad1, pad_01, and pad_08 with offsets where available.
Temporary/local names in the diff often encode observed role rather than final semantics, such as gobj1_slot, gobj2_slot, strip_h, neg_y, neg_yh, localAxis, rotMtx, and resultMtx.
HSD and Dolphin SDK types are used directly once identified: GXColor, HSD_GObj, HSD_CObj, HSD_JObj, Quaternion, Mtx, MtxPtr, and Vec3.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK with explicit sizes in several functions, including fn_8001FEC4, lbBgFlash_800209F4, fn_8002113C, fn_80021B04, and lbBgFlash_80021410.
Used #pragma dont_inline around lbBgFlash_800208EC/fn_80021B04 and #pragma inline_depth around matrix helpers to influence inlining.
Kept fn_8001FC08 manually unrolled for four RGBA channels; the author comment says trying to un-unroll made it remain a loop in asm.
Used explicit __frsqrte Newton refinement sequences instead of ordinary sqrt calls for vector lengths and normalization.
Used volatile f32 temporaries in lbBgFlash_80020E38 and explicit f64/f32 casts in math-heavy code to shape floating-point codegen.
Inserted no-op-looking conditions such as repeated pointer truth checks in some functions, likely as codegen shims; evidence is from the diff, not reviewer explanation.
Used byte-level access to GXColor in fn_80021B04 to match stores to individual color bytes.
Preserved exact constants visible in the diff, including 640.0f, -480.0f, 0x280, 0x1E0, pi-like constants, 0.1745329201221466, and 2.9670597334676465.
Used local fake_HSD_ASSERT and fake HSD_JObj rotation accessors to mirror expected inline/assert behavior in fn_8002113C.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
PR body only said: "Tracking PR as per usual."
decomp.dev bot reported matched code rising to 59.17% (+0.04%, +1448 bytes).
decomp.dev bot reported 5 new full matches: lbBgFlash_800206D4, fn_80021B04, lbBgFlash_800208EC, lbBgFlash_80021A18, and lbBgFlash_800209F4.

decomp.dev bot reported 8 unmatched-item improvements, notably fn_8001FEC4 to 91.15%, lbBgFlash_80021410 to 81.06%, lbBgFlash_80020E38 to 98.46%, fn_80020AEC to 88.27%, fn_8002113C to 93.17%, and fn_8001FC08 to 89.68%.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that finish fn_8001FEC4, fn_8001FC08, fn_80020AEC, fn_8002113C, lbBgFlash_80020E38, or lbBgFlash_80021410 after this partial match.
Search for later renames of BgFlashData x-fields once background flash semantics were better understood.
Check whether fake_HSD_ASSERT or fake HSD_JObj rotation wrappers were later replaced by shared baselib inline definitions.
Search for other lbbgflash PRs involving LbBf.dat or lbBgFlashColAnimData to connect ColorOverlay data layout and command animation parsing.
